core_mechanism: a control-parameter-tuned critical transition between two ground states of a many-body system, set by the competition of two energy scales (a delocalizing coupling/hopping term versus a localizing on-site penalty), where crossing a critical ratio switches the system from a globally coherent, delocalized phase to a localized phase with a gap in its excitation spectrum
brief_reason: The abstract describes a zero-temperature transition induced by varying the relative strength of two competing energy terms across a critical value, taking the ground state from a delocalized, long-range phase-coherent (superfluid) state to a localized, gapped (Mott-insulator) state.
